title: Whitepaper detail
summary: Long-form whitepapers run 20k+ pixels in v1 with no TOC. v2 adds Executive Summary as self-contained artifact, in-page TOC with anchor links, section progress affordance.

strategy.decision: Rule 5 (progressive disclosure): every whitepaper opens with a self-contained Executive Summary that reads as a complete artifact. The TOC + anchor links + section progress are progressive disclosure — readers can stop at the Exec Summary and have gotten the argument, or dive into specific sections.
strategy.why: v1-baseline/01-diagnose.md F4: whitepaper-detail at 1440 runs ~20k px tall with no within-page nav. Peer-architect persona's primary drop-pattern is bailing rather than scroll-hunting. Competitive support: Stratechery section headers, Pragmatic Engineer deep-dive TOCs, charity.wtf anchor-link headers all do this. v1 is the outlier in the peer set.
strategy.shipped: v1 whitepapers: plain markdown rendered top-to-bottom, no TOC, no section anchors, table-heavy formatting. Voice + framing are strong per diagnose; the navigability layer is missing.
strategy.gap: v2 whitepaper detail adds: (a) self-contained Executive Summary, (b) clickable TOC at top with anchor links, (c) section-progress indicator (e.g., '3 of 8' sticky on scroll), (d) preserves v1's table-heavy formatting and plain-markdown discipline.
strategy.question: Funnel gap G3 (engagement depth measurement) is open — if readers turn out to bounce at the Exec Summary, the rest-of-doc affordances are lower priority. Doesn't block build; affects post-launch instrumentation.

inheritsFromV1: plain markdown (no MDX in whitepapers — convention)
companionOf binding to a parent post

currentState.summary: v1 whitepaper: plain markdown, ~20k px tall, no TOC, no anchor links, no progress affordance.
currentState.annotation: v2 adds the navigation layer v1 is missing while preserving the formal voice + table-heavy content. Progressive disclosure (Rule 5) is the specific principle this surface tests.
